commit.message: Keep Running...

Add custom scrollbars and bump version

Style horizontal tab navs with visible, themed scrollbars (thin width, colored track/thumb, hover state), change overflow-x from auto to scroll, and add bottom padding for spacing. Apply same scrollbar styling to manage-tabs. Bump plugin version to 1.3.5. Files changed: ConfigUiHelper.php, manage-tabs.php, Plugin.php.
Update hitokoto data and rules [skip ci]

Use touch-action for tab scrolling

Replace custom pointer-based drag-to-scroll logic with native touch panning. Added touch-action: pan-x to the tab scrollers and removed the enableDragScroll function, its event handlers, and .is-dragging styles from ConfigUiHelper.php and manage-tabs.php. Simplifies code by relying on native touch scrolling and removes manual cursor, click-suppression and pointer-capture handling.
Update github-data.json

Improve tab scroller drag behavior

Add click-suppression and refine drag handling for scrollers. Introduces a suppressClick flag to prevent clicks after a drag, increases movement threshold from 3 to 6px to reduce accidental drags, ignores non-mouse pointer types, and consolidates scrollLeft updates. Changes applied to ConfigUiHelper.php and manage-tabs.php to reduce unintended clicks/scrolls on tab scrollers and improve mouse drag responsiveness.

Enable drag-to-scroll tabs and harden S3 handling

Add touch/drag-to-scroll support for plugin/manage tabs and settings nav (CSS, data-enhancement-drag-scroll attribute and pointer-based JS with grab/grabbing, click suppression and debounce). Prevent unregistering S3 hooks during init in ConfigStorageHelper. Harden S3 helper by extracting export/init logic into exportPluginHandles() and initPluginHandles() with class/method existence checks and exception safety, validate returned data, use a single json_encode result when persisting, and ensure the DB update targets user = 0. Overall improves mobile UX for tab lists and makes plugin handle export/init/persistence more robust against Typecho runtime issues.

Repair core plugins option on activation

Add repairCorePluginsOption() (invoked in activate()) to ensure a valid 'plugins' option exists and is normalized. It reads existing option rows, decodes JSON or serialized values, ensures 'activated' and 'handles' arrays, updates mismatched rows, and inserts a default global (user=0) row if missing. Added helper encode/decode functions which prefer JSON when Typecho\Plugin is present and fall back to serialize. Exceptions are caught to avoid breaking activation.
